title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Program Brief: Migration Diff
summary: The newest artifact replaces production without a route diff, removed URLs are discovered by readers, rollback requires rebuilding, caches preserve broken assets, ownership ends after deployment, and generated volume is called completion. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the migration diff increment in production publication, migration, and reco…
objectives: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.
Verify representative URLs, headers, assets, fragments, accessibility tasks, canonical origin, cache behavior, artifact identity, and rollback trigger after publication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Migration Diff · Theory Migration Diff
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 3671 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Migration Diff contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example publishing team handles case 3671 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Migration Diff, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production publication, migration, and recovery dossier.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_release_recovery_defense_4(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Migration Diff evidence · Theory Migration Diff

Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-migration-diff-6qlhcc
# Competency: ssg-migration-diff.
# Case 3850: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_migration_diff_6qlhcc(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_6qlhcc = "6qlhcc-cchlq6-3850"
    assert evidence_variant_6qlhcc
    evidence_axes_6qlhcc = (
        "reader6qlhcc",
        "source3850",
        "parsecchlq6",
        "route6qlhcc3850",
        "artifact38506qlhcc",
        "recoverycchlq63850",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_6qlhcc)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Migration Diff model · Theory Migration Diff
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Migration Diff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 4385 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.
